headline: FINRA Wants Broke Kids to Blow Up Their Margin Accounts Faster
summary: Regulators are getting ready to drop the pattern day trading threshold from $25,000 to $2,000, opening the door for a wave of new traders on Robinhood, others.

headline: John Arnold Criticism Puts Robinhood Betting Pivot And Valuation In Focus
summary: Billionaire investor John Arnold has publicly criticized Robinhood Markets' move into prediction and sports betting markets. Arnold is warning about regulatory gaps, addiction risk, and the risk of blurring investing with gambling for younger users. He is calling for tighter rules, including stronger age verification and limits on how these products are marketed. Robinhood Markets (NasdaqGS:HOOD), trading at $86.85, is drawing fresh attention as prediction markets become a bigger part of...

headline: Coinbase and Strategy Rise as Bitcoin Hits $76,000 on Cease-Fire Hopes
summary: Bitcoin  and other cryptocurrencies rose Friday amid signs of easing tensions across the Middle East.  Strategy  the largest corporate holder of Bitcoin, rose 4.4%; online trading platform  Robinhood  climbed 4.1%; and crypto exchange  Coinbase  was up 1.8%.  After weeks of fighting between Israel and the Iran-backed militant group Hezbollah, Israel and Lebanon agreed to a 10-day cease-fire on Thursday, according to The Wall Street Journal.

headline: Billionaire Investor Warns Sports Prediction Markets Harm Men
summary: (Bloomberg) -- John Arnold, the billionaire energy trader-turned-philanthropist, made his fortune by predicting the direction of markets. Now, he’s sounding the alarm on fast-growing prediction markets, saying they’re harmful to young men and boys.His concern lies in how platforms for sports-event contracts and online betting more broadly are designed to keep users constantly engaged. Unlike traditional forms of gambling, wagers can be placed seamlessly — quickly and easily through mobile apps w
